verses.commentary: Cried out: Have everyone leave! No one with Joseph when made himself known. Emotion overwhelms. Clears the room. Private moment.
Wept so loudly. Public knowledge.
The revelation. Direct announcement. Brothers speechless. They sold him. What will he do?
The one you sold into Egypt. You sold me.
Reframes the story. Not you sent me, God sent me. Divine purpose. Their sin became God's tool.
Timeline given. Famine halfway done. Seven years total. Worse to come.
To preserve remnant. Third time: God sent me. Preserve remnant (she'erit, שְׁאֵרִית). God's covenant promises at stake.
But God. Lord of household. Fourth time: God sent, not you. Father to Pharaoh (advisor). Lord and ruler. Complete authority. God's sovereignty in their evil.
Hurry to father. Come down. Don't delay. Urgent invitation. Message for Jacob. Your son Joseph. Still his son. Come to Egypt. Time sensitive.
Live in Goshen. Be near me. Practical plan. Goshen region. Proximity. Whole family. All possessions. Total relocation.
I will provide. Otherwise destitute. Joseph's provision promised. Five more years. Salvation offered.
You can see. Benjamin can see. Really I speaking. Benjamin's eyes. Proof of identity.
Show evidence. Urgent: bring Jacob. Quickly.
Wept. Mutual weeping. Deep emotion.
Kissed all brothers. Wept over them. Tears for each. Barriers dissolve.
Public knowledge. Royal approval.
Pharaoh joins in.
Royal invitation. Best land promised. Prosperity offered. Fat of land. Abundance.
Carts for family. Practical help. Remove obstacles.
Leave it behind. Upgrade coming.
Compliance. Generosity.
Five sets. Favoritism shown.
Lavish gifts. Best of Egypt.
As leaving said: Don't quarrel on way! Joseph knows them. Prone to conflict. Wise warning. Keep peace.
Return journey.
Jacob is stunned. The news is impossible. Joseph alive? Ruling Egypt? Too good to be true. His heart can't process it. Unbelief from hope deferred (Proverbs 13:12).
Spirit revived. Carts are evidence. Physical proof. Spirit revived (literally came back to life).
Faith returns. Still his son. Before death.
